volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: The Comedy of Asses (Section 2.2.331 - Section 2.3.403)

sections.label: Section 2.2.331
sections.original: Mitto . ⋯ istuc quod adfers aures exspectant meae .
sections.translation: I ’ ll have done ; for my ears are in expectation of what you are bringing me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.332
sections.original: Animum adverte , ut aeque mecum haéc scias .
sections.translation: Give your attention , that equally with myself you may learn this .

sections.label: Section 2.2.332b
sections.translation: I ’ m silent , then .

sections.label: Section 2.2.332c
sections.translation: You oblige me . Don ’ t you remember that our chamberlain sold some Arcadian asses to a dealer of Pella [*] ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.334_335b
sections.translation: I remember it ; after that , what then ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.336
sections.original: Em ergo is argentum huc remisit , quod daretur Saureae
sections.translation: Well , he has sent some money here then to be paid to Saurea , for the asses ; a young man has just now come who has brought this money .

sections.label: Section 2.2.338
sections.translation: Where is this person ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.338b
sections.translation: You think he ought to be devoured this instant , if you could see him .

sections.label: Section 2.2.339
sections.original: Ita enim vero . sed tamen , tu nempe eos asinos praedicas
sections.translation: Aye , to be sure . But , however , you are speaking , I suppose , of those asses ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.340
sections.original: vetulos , claudos , quibus subtritae ad femina iám erant ungulae ?
sections.translation: aged and lame , whose hoofs were quite worn away to their very thighs ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.341
sections.original: Ipsos , qui tibi subvectabant rure huc virgas ulmeas .
sections.translation: Those same ones , that carried the elm twigs hither from the country , for your use .

sections.label: Section 2.2.342
sections.original: Teneo , atque idem te hinc vexerunt vinctum rus .
sections.translation: I understand you ; and the same ones carried you from here , bound , into the country [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.2.342b
sections.translation: You say what ’ s quite correct . But as I was sitting in the barber ’ s shop , he began to make enquiries of me , whether I knew a certain Demaenetus , the son of Strato .

sections.label: Section 2.2.345
sections.original: dico me novisse extemplo et me eius servom praedico
sections.translation: At once I said that I knew him , and that I was his servant ; and I pointed out our house .

sections.label: Section 2.2.346b
sections.translation: After that , what then ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.347
sections.original: Ait se ob asinos ferre argentum átriensi Saureae ,
sections.translation: He said that he was bringing the money for the asses to the chamberlain Saurea , twenty minae in amount ; but that he himself didn ’ t know the individual , who he was , but that he knew Demaenetus quite well .

sections.label: Section 2.2.350
sections.translation: Since he spoke thus to this effect —

sections.label: Section 2.2.350b
sections.translation: What then ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.350c
sections.translation: Listen then , and you ’ ll know . At once I made myself courteous , and a person of consequence . I said that I was the chamberlain . Thus , in these terms did he answer me : Upon my faith , I don ’ t know Saurea , nor yet of what appearance he is . It isn ’ t fair for you to blame me ; but if you like , bring here Demaenetus , your master , whom I do know ; I ’ ll not prevent you taking the money then .

sections.label: Section 2.2.356
sections.original: ego me dixi erum adducturum et me domi praesto fore ;
sections.translation: I said that I would bring him , and that I should be at home immediately . He ’ s about to go to the baths [*] , from there he ’ ll afterwards come here . What plan do you think , now , I ought to adopt ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.358b
sections.translation: Why , I ’ m thinking of this , how to get between the money , and the stranger , and Saurea .

sections.label: Section 2.2.360
sections.original: iam hoc opus est † exasceatum ; nam si ille argentum prius
sections.translation: At present this matter is rough-hewn ; but if this stranger brings here the money first , then are we both at once shut out from it . But the old man to-day took me apart at a distance from the house , and threatened me and yourself that we should be tasters of the elm twigs , if Argyrippus didn ’ t this very day get twenty mine of silver .

sections.label: Section 2.2.365
sections.original: iussit vel nos atriensem vel nos uxorem suam
sections.translation: He commanded that we should cheat either the chamberlain or his own wife , and said that he would give the aid he promised . Now , do you go to the Forum to our master , and tell him this , how we are going to manage ; that you , from Leonida , are going to be the chamberlain Saurea , until the dealer has brought the money for the asses .

sections.label: Section 2.2.369b
sections.translation: I ’ ll do as you request me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.370
sections.original: Ego illum interea hic oblectabo , prius si forte advenerit .
sections.translation: In the meantime , I ’ ll amuse him here , if by chance he should come first .

sections.label: Section 2.2.371
sections.translation: But what say you —?

sections.label: Section 2.2.371b
sections.translation: What do you want ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.371c
sections.translation: If I give you a blow on the cheek with my fist , by-and-by , while I ’ m personating Saurea [*] , don ’ t you be offended .

sections.label: Section 2.2.373
sections.original: Hercle vero tu cavebis ne me attingas , si sapis ,
sections.translation: I ’ faith , but you ’ ll have a care not to be touching me , if you are wise ; you ’ ll surely have changed your name to day with a bad omen [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.2.375
sections.translation: Prithee , do endure it with resolution .

sections.label: Section 2.2.375b
sections.original: Patitor tú item , cum ego te referiam .
sections.translation: Do you endure the cuff that I , too , shall be giving you in return .

sections.label: Section 2.2.376
sections.translation: I speak as it ’ s in the habit of being done .

sections.label: Section 2.2.376b
sections.original: Dico hercle ego quoque ut facturus sum .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , and I speak , too , of how I ’ m likely to act .

sections.label: Section 2.2.377
sections.translation: Don ’ t refuse me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.377b
sections.original: Quin promitto , inquam , hostire contra ut merueris .
sections.translation: Why I promise , I tell you , to give you a like return , just as you deserve .

sections.label: Section 2.2.378
sections.original: Ego abeo , tu iam , scio , patiere . sed quis hic est ? is est ,
sections.translation: I ’ m off ; I know that you ’ ll put up with it by-and-by . But who ’ s this ? ’ Tis he —’ tis the very man himself . I ’ ll return here just now ; in the meantime do you detain him here ;

sections.label: Section 2.2.380
sections.translation: I want to inform the old gentleman .

sections.label: Section 2.2.380b
sections.original: Quin tuom officium facis ergo ac fugis ?
sections.translation: Well , do your duty , then , and fly .

sections.label: Section 2.3.381
sections.original: Vt démonstratae súnt mihi , hasce aédis esse opórtet ,
sections.translation: According as it was pointed out to me , this must be the house where Demaenetus is said to live . ( To the BOY .) Go , boy , and knock , and call Saurea the chamberlain out here , if he ’ s in-doors .

sections.label: Section 2.3.384
sections.original: Quis nostras sic frangit fores ? ohe , inquam , si quid audis .
sections.translation: Who ’ s breaking in our door in this fashion ? Enough there , I say , if you hear me at all .

sections.label: Section 2.3.385
sections.translation: No one has touched it as yet : are you out of your senses ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.385b
sections.translation: Why I thought that you had touched it , because you were steering your course in that direction . I don ’ t want the door , my fellow-slave [*] , to be thumped by you ; I really am attached to our house .

sections.label: Section 2.3.388
sections.original: Pol haud periclum est , cardines ne foribus effringantur ,
sections.translation: I ’ faith , there ’ s no fear of the hinges being broken off the doors , if you answer all who make enquiries in this fashion .

sections.label: Section 2.3.390
sections.original: Ita haec morata est ianua : extemplo ianitorem
sections.translation: This door is of this habit ; it cries out at once for tho porter , if it sees any door-kicker at a distance coming towards it . But what are you come for ? What are you enquiring about ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.392b
sections.translation: I wanted Demaenetus .

sections.label: Section 2.3.393
sections.translation: If he were at home , I would tell you so .

sections.label: Section 2.3.393b
sections.translation: Well , his chamberlain then ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.394
sections.translation: No more is ne at home .

sections.label: Section 2.3.394b
sections.translation: Where is he ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.394c
sections.translation: He said he was going to the barber ’ s .

sections.label: Section 2.3.395
sections.translation: Hasn ’ t he returned , since he went there ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.395b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , he hasn ’ t . What did you want ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.396
sections.original: Argenti viginti minas , si adesset , accepisset .
sections.translation: He was to have received twenty mine of silver , if he had been in .

sections.label: Section 2.3.397
sections.translation: What was it for ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.397b
sections.translation: He sold some asses at market to a dealer from Pella .

sections.label: Section 2.3.398b
sections.original: Scio . tu id nunc refers ? iam hic credo eum adfuturum .
sections.translation: I understand ; you are bringing it now . I think that he ’ ll be here just now .

sections.label: Section 2.3.399
sections.original: Qua facie voster Saurea est ? si is est , iam scire potero .
sections.translation: Of what appearance is your chamberlain Saurea ? If it ’ s he , I shall be able to know at once .

sections.label: Section 2.3.400
sections.translation: Lantern-jawed , with reddish hair , a little pot-bellied , with glaring eyes , middling stature , sour aspect .

sections.label: Section 2.3.402
sections.original: Non potuit pictor rectius describere eius formam .
sections.translation: A painter couldn ’ t have more correctly described his appearance — And , i ’ faith , I see the very man ; he ’ s coming this way , wagging his head .

sections.label: Section 2.3.403
sections.original: Atque hercle ipsum adeo contuor , quassanti capite incedit .
sections.translation: Whoever gets in his way when he ’ s in a passion , he ’ ll be for striking him .
